name: International Aid Charity
mission: International Aid Charity (IAC) provides emergency and ongoing aid relief, education, orphan sponsorship and social support services to marginalized peoples throughout the world. Our donors and volunteers work directly with communities to offer both physical and emotional health through education, housing and vocational training for orphans and widows, sports and creative arts programs. IAC offers a foundation of hope and resources upon which those in need can build and thrive.
theoryOfChange: Website states it provides schooling, health care, emotional support for widows and orphans and vocational training to empower people to build their own future, and offers a foundation of hope and resources upon which those in need can build and thrive.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Scoring 43/50 in alignment, the organization offers high donor fit for those prioritizing humanitarian urgency and Islamic giving principles [2]. The charity facilitates various giving methods, including zakat and general donations, to support its mission of aiding the marginalized [2][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity achieves a high impact score of 48/50 by maintaining a program expense ratio of 87.8% and a low cost per beneficiary [1][3]. Its direct service model in orphan sponsorship and emergency relief ensures that its total revenue of $851,150 is utilized effectively with high data confidence [1].

strengths.detail: The organization spends <cite id="5">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in contribution revenue, which is significantly better than industry averages.
IAC maintains <cite id="10">4.4 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, ensuring stability during economic or regional volatility.
The charity earned <cite id="11">perfect scores of 100 in both Culture and Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator.

strengths_deep_dive: International Aid Charity excels in financial stewardship and organizational culture. Their <cite id="12">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> indicates a high level of public disclosure. Furthermore, the organization manages to provide services at a highly competitive rate, with an estimated <cite id="13">cost of $49.53 per beneficiary</cite> in their humanitarian programs. This efficiency is coupled with a <cite id="14">documented theory of change</cite> that guides their emergency food and trauma-informed education initiatives.
rich_narrative.summary: International Aid Charity (IAC) provides essential humanitarian relief and social support to marginalized communities through highly efficient financial management. The organization has demonstrated rapid growth, achieving a <cite id="1">3-year revenue CAGR of 69.7%</cite>, with total revenue reaching <cite id="2">$1,481,959 in 2023</cite>. IAC operates a diverse portfolio of programs including <cite id="3">orphan sponsorship, refugee schools, and emergency relief</cite> across multiple countries such as Gaza, Syria, and Afghanistan. The charity maintains an <cite id="4">overall score of 87.87.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting a strong commitment to accountability and leadership. A defining feature of their financial model is their <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that donor contributions are not eroded by high solicitation costs. While the organization reported a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 87.8%</cite> in its most recent filing, other rating agencies note a <cite id="7">program expense ratio of 87.8%</cite>, suggesting a high volume of direct service delivery. The organization is <cite id="8">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the categories of <cite id="9">fuqara (the poor), masakin (the needy), and ibn al-sabil (the wayfarer)</cite>. With <cite id="10">4.4 months of working capital</cite>, IAC is positioned as a resilient institution capable of sustaining operations during humanitarian crises.
